title: OpenSSL-internal (not CPython): concurrent ssl.SSLContext()/SSL_CTX_new races OpenSSL's global algorithm-fetch cache; surfaces under tp_new_wrapper
description: The seeded `tp_new_wrapper | tp_new_wrapper` signature is a symbolization artifact: the actual race is memcmp(read)/memcpy(write) on a 104-byte CRYPTO_malloc'd block INSIDE libcrypto.so.3 (OpenSSL 3.5.5). Two threads each independently construct their OWN ssl.SSLContext -> _ssl._SSLContext.__new__ (tp_new_wrapper, typeobject.c:10478) -> SSL_CTX_new, which populates OpenSSL's process-global method/algorithm-fetch cache: locked memcpy insert vs lock-free memcmp lookup. No CPython object or type state races. Not a CPython bug.
sites: Objects/typeobject.c tp_new_wrapper:10478 (res = type->tp_new(subtype, args_tuple, kwds) -> _ssl._SSLContext C tp_new -> SSL_CTX_new; NEAREST SYMBOLIZED frame only)
libcrypto.so.3+0x250b01 (read: memcmp on OpenSSL method-store/cache entry; lock-free lookup) -- UNSYMBOLIZED OpenSSL internals
libcrypto.so.3+0x250916 (write: memcpy populating OpenSSL cache entry; holds CRYPTO_THREAD lock M0) -- UNSYMBOLIZED OpenSSL internals
libcrypto.so.3+0x261d65 CRYPTO_malloc (allocates the racing 104-byte OpenSSL heap block)
libcrypto.so.3+0x276ec5 CRYPTO_THREAD_lock_new / +0x276fac CRYPTO_THREAD_run_once (creates mutex M0 during module init)
race_kind: third-party (OpenSSL/libcrypto) internal cache-population race: locked memcpy insert vs lock-free memcmp lookup; reached via legitimate independent object construction. NOT a CPython race.
shared_state: OpenSSL process-global algorithm-fetch / method-store cache entry (104-byte CRYPTO_malloc'd block in libcrypto's EVP/provider layer). NOT a PyObject, NOT CPython type state.
severity: none for CPython (not a CPython bug); low for OpenSSL (idempotent first-touch cache population, torn 8-byte read, very likely benign / known upstream)
repro_reliability: reliable: 6/6 with NT=24 + per-round barrier (exit 66). Race window is the single cold-cache first-touch; a naive 8-thread version hits ~1/3.
suggested_fix: None in CPython. Add a fleet suppression (like TSAN-0003's glibc entry in catalog/suppressions.txt) keyed on tp_new_wrapper|tp_new_wrapper GATED on memcmp/memcpy top frames in libcrypto.so.3 (bare tp_new_wrapper key is too broad). Optionally add libcrypto to Tools/tsan/suppressions_free_threading.txt. Optional FYI to OpenSSL; do NOT include in the CPython umbrella.
scope_decision: OUT OF SCOPE. NOT concurrent construction of the same shared object (cpython#127192) -- each thread builds its OWN distinct SSLContext, nothing shared at the Python level. NOT a race on shared CPython type-level state (tp_flags/version-tag/__new__ cache/ht_cached_keys/mro) -- the racing address is OpenSSL heap (CRYPTO_malloc), and both accesses are memcmp/memcpy inside libcrypto. It is a data race INSIDE OpenSSL, reached through a documented thread-safe entry point (SSL_CTX_new). tp_new_wrapper is only the nearest symbolized frame because libcrypto is stripped. Disposition mirrors TSAN-0003 (third-party lib race under a CPython frame), though here the write holds a CRYPTO_THREAD rwlock TSan sees and the read is genuinely lock-free (a real OpenSSL race, not a pure TSan artifact).
notes: Every libcrypto offset in the confirmed repro matches the seeded vehicle report byte-for-byte (memcmp +0x250b01, memcpy +0x250916, CRYPTO_malloc +0x261d65, CRYPTO_THREAD_lock_new +0x276ec5). Trigger chain: ssl.SSLContext.__new__ (Lib/ssl.py:438, slot_tp_new) -> _ssl._SSLContext.__new__ (tp_new_wrapper) -> SSL_CTX_new. ANY OpenSSL-backed construction/first-touch (ssl/hashlib/hmac) collapses to this same tp_new_wrapper frame, so the signature is a poor dedup key and should be suppressed gated on the libcrypto frames.

related_issues.note: concurrent construction of the SAME object is unsupported -- does NOT apply here: each thread constructs its own distinct SSLContext; scope decision is out-of-scope for a different reason (OpenSSL-internal race).

assessment: not a CPython bug (OpenSSL-internal / third-party); reproduced and root-caused; out of scope for the CPython FT umbrella
disposition: OpenSSL-internal race (libcrypto method-store cache), NOT CPython -- tp_new_wrapper is just the nearest symbolized frame. Like TSAN-0003. Signatures[] emptied so it stays OUT of known_races.tsv (tp_new_wrapper|tp_new_wrapper is too broad -- would mislabel any OpenSSL-backed construction). Documented; resurfaces as NEW (acceptable).
